Completion Time : Model Selection

CongLiu

1 1. Data import and preprocessing

# ---- Data location ----
# The raw dataset is stored outside this analysis folder (e.g., in a sibling directory).
# If your folder layout differs, update `candidate_dirs` below.
data_file <- "Experimental_Raw_Data.csv"
candidate_dirs <- c(
  file.path("..", "data"),
  file.path("..", "datasets"),
  ".."
)
candidate_paths <- file.path(candidate_dirs, data_file)
data_path <- candidate_paths[file.exists(candidate_paths)][1]

if (is.na(data_path)) {
  stop("Data file not found. Checked:
  - ", paste(candidate_paths, collapse = "
  - "))
}

dat <- readr::read_csv(data_path, show_col_types = FALSE) %>% 
  mutate(
    group       = factor(group, levels = c("3D-only","2D+3D")),
    complexity  = factor(complexity, levels = c("simple","complex")),
    participant = factor(participant),
    log_ct      = log(completion_time)
  )

2 2. Candidate model specification and fitting

Model ID Random-effects structure Heteroscedasticity Implementation
M0 (1 | participant) No lmer
M1 (1 + complexity | participant) No lmer
M2 (1 | participant) varIdent(~1 | complexity) nlme::lme
M3 (1 + complexity | participant) varIdent(~1 | complexity) nlme::lme 
# M0: random intercept
m0 <- lmer(log_ct ~ group * complexity + (1 | participant), data = dat, REML = TRUE)

# M1: random slope
m1 <- lmer(log_ct ~ group * complexity + (1 + complexity | participant), data = dat, REML = TRUE)

# M2: heteroscedastic (by complexity)
m2 <- lme(log_ct ~ group * complexity,
          random  = ~1 | participant,
          weights = varIdent(form = ~1 | complexity),
          data = dat,
          method = "REML")

# M3: random slope + heteroscedastic
m3 <- lme(log_ct ~ group * complexity,
          random  = ~1 + complexity | participant,
          weights = varIdent(form = ~1 | complexity),
          data = dat,
          control = lmeControl(returnObject = TRUE),
          method = "REML")

3 3. Model comparison

models <- list(M0 = m0, M1 = m1, M2 = m2, M3 = m3)
performance::compare_performance(models, rank = TRUE)
## Random effect variances not available. Returned R2 does not account for random effects.
## Random effect variances not available. Returned R2 does not account for random effects.
AICtab <- data.frame(
  Model = names(models),
  AIC   = sapply(models, AIC),
  BIC   = sapply(models, BIC)
)
knitr::kable(AICtab, digits = 1)
Model AIC BIC
M0 M0 -147.7 -124.0
M1 M1 -144.0 -112.4
M2 M2 -161.0 -133.4
M3 M3 -157.0 -121.5

4 4. Diagnostics (best model: M2)

performance::check_model(m2)

Decision. Based on compare_performance and AIC/BIC, M2 achieved the best overall performance; therefore, all subsequent analyses use M2 (random intercepts with a complexity-specific variance structure).
